# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
import { decode } from 'jsonwebtoken';

// Define a type for the decoded JWT payload
interface DecodedTokenPayload {
  userId?: string;
  email?: string;
  name?: string;
  // Add other properties you expect in your JWT payload
  [key: string]: any;
}

// Extend the Express Request type to include a user property
declare global {
  namespace Express {
    interface Request {
      user?: DecodedTokenPayload;
    }
  }
}

export const jwtDecodeMiddleware = (req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
  const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;

  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith('Bearer ')) {
    return res.status(401).json({ message: 'No token provided or token format is incorrect.' });
  }

  const token = authHeader.split(' ')[1]; // Extract the token string

  try {
    // Decode the token without verification
    const decodedPayload = decode(token) as DecodedTokenPayload | null;

    if (!decodedPayload) {
      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid token.' });
    }

    // Attach the decoded user information to the request object
    req.user = decodedPayload;

    next();
  } catch (error) {
    // This catch block will primarily handle errors from `decode` if the token is not a valid JWT format
    console.error('Error decoding JWT:', error);
    return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Failed to decode token.', error: (error as Error).message });
  }
};
